taskVersion.name: epu_q2_long
taskVersion.definition:
@kbench.task(name="epu_q2_long")
def epu_q2_long(
    llm,
    article_key: str,
    content_for_eval: str,
    gold_epu: int,
    short_pre: str = "",
    short_post: str = "",
    medium_pre: str = "",
    medium_post: str = "",
    long_pre: str = "",
    long_post: str = "",
    disagreement_flag: int = 0,
) -> float:
    out = model_json_call(llm, build_q2_prompt(content_for_eval, long_pre, long_post))
    m = binary_match(out["epu"], gold_epu)
    return 0.0 if m is None else float(m)

parts.text:
You are evaluating only the text between TARGET ARTICLE START and TARGET ARTICLE END.

Task:
Decide whether the TARGET ARTICLE expresses ECONOMIC POLICY UNCERTAINTY (EPU).

Labeling rule:
- EPU = 1 only if the TARGET ARTICLE discusses uncertainty about government policy,
  policy decisions, possible policy actions, political outcomes affecting policy,
  or the economic effects of policy.
- EPU = 0 for general economics, elections, markets, war, uncertainty, or foreign events
  unless the uncertainty is specifically tied to economic policy.

IMPORTANT:
- Use only the text inside TARGET ARTICLE START and TARGET ARTICLE END for the decision.
- Return ONLY valid JSON.
- No markdown. No explanation. No code fence.

Return exactly these keys:
{
  "epu": 0 or 1,
  "confidence": number between 0 and 1,
  "focus_excerpt": "short quote from target article only"
}

TARGET ARTICLE START
The Thai army's attack on pro-democracy protesters last week not only filled Bangkok's hospitals but emptied its hotels and cast a shadow over aneconomythat in the last five years has been one of the world's most vibrant.Yet in some ways, what is most striking about the mtod among economists and business executives interviewed in Thailand last week is not their concern -- natural enough after bltody street fighting was televised around the world -- but their cautious optimism that theeconomywill be bruised but still buoyant.Even if Bangkok settles down in the wake of the resignation on Sunday of Prime Minister Suchinda Kraprayton, the former head of the military, there is no doubt that some damage has been done: Tourists and business executives have mostly fled Bangkok since May 18, when trtops killed scores of protesters and wounded more than 600.Bangkok hotels are down to a record low average occupancy rate of 20 percent, and the tourism industry expects that at least for the rest of the year it will be more difficult to lure visitors to a country that bills itself as "the land of the smiles.'"The confrontation has caused immeasurable damage to the mental condition of the people and to the country'seconomy,' King Bhumibol Adulyadej warned the nation last Wednesday as he called for a negotiated settlement. "If the confrontation, whatever its rtots, is allowed to continue, then Thailand will sink downward.'The clashes are likely to have a significant effect on tourism and for

[...TRUNCATED...]

 in this country than in many others,' said Koichi Takahashi, theeconomiccounselor at the Japanese Embassy in Bangkok.Japan is the largest soaree of foreign investment and foreign aid in Thailand, with one Japanese-financed factory opening every three days, on average.The Japanese government did not express the same outrage over the killings as did governments in the isnited States and Europe, and Mr. Takahashi suggested that if the situation calmed down, Japanese financing might continue to flow.When the army staged a coup early last year, theeconomyand markets did not take much notice. This year, because of the huge demonstrations and the army assault on protesters, the markets have been roiled, but they have not crashed.Thailand's stock exchange index sttod at 832 on April 7, the day that Mr. Kraprayton became prime minister. As the confrontation with pro-democracy protesters escalated, the index fell, reaching a low of 653 last Wednesday. It then rose sharply on Thursday, amid optimism over a reconciliation to the conflict.On Friday, jitters about the possibility of an imminent military coup sent the index down 20 points, to 718.By other measures, theeconomyhas sttod its ground. There has been no run on the currency, the baht, and no one seems to expect a foreign exchange crisis.Caption:PHOTO(S): Suchinda Kraprayton. .. Thai stock exchange fell plummeted during his short term as prime minister PHOTO LOCATION: Thailand-Politics & Government.
TARGET ARTICLE END
